subject: just announced: my big idea
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Julian for the Future
body:
| | |
---
| | [](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcdce/3eb3e2fc/416462f0/74494586/734429202/VEsH/ "https://www.julianforthefuture.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=201900601_em_pol-policing&source=201900601_em_pol-policing")
---
| | I just got off the stage at **MoveOn's #BigIdeas forum** where I presented my BIG idea to improve the lives of Americans across the country. We need big, bold ideas that address systemic issues and spark change. We also need a president who's not afraid to tackle these issues head-on. **That 's why today I laid out my vision to end racially discriminatory policing and mend the relationship between local police and the communities they serve.** My plan would:
* Introduce new policing guidelines that would demilitarize police departments and require police to deescalate an incident before using deadly force.
* Hold police accountable for these deaths and use of excessive force.
* Start the healing process between communities and law enforcement.

Watch a clip of my pitch below, and stay tuned for a full release of my **Policing Reform** plan this Monday. [](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcdce/3eb3e2fc/416462f0/74494585/734429202/VEsE/ "https://twitter.com/JulianCastro/status/1134963185725743104?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=201900601_em_pol-policing&source=201900601_em_pol-policing") More to come,
Julian **[DONATE](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcdce/3eb3e2fc/416462f0/74494584/734429202/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jcgen?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=3+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=201900601_em_pol-policing&source=201900601_em_pol-policing")**

name: John Kasich
subject: Two years
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kasich for America, not authorized by any
body:
Saturday was not a milestone to be proud about, but it is an opportunity to point out that retreating from big challenges is not the American way [ ](http://links.johnkasich.com/u/click?_t=6a3b28686d0a4d77958e12349305a4fe&_m=9b757025c5fc4b13a928493bb3349211&_e=LnIBVQq_c_-DzD0YV6REK8WU-ArPLoELEwdgwZnA7aPSC7pViX61Xyc_fGzSIxeTZJGGNlwM7iCZKA1NMrnV-H-WbQifI_HCrLzC1Pja7dR9SPE8V6fx42wRBZ_jImSjZF1Rm0NqL7Hj4S8KNLOdLHr6t6tICVcha7pcl6X4vClnE7r77bLN359FebmkcoBoHeU0L1hRaRQMDaa8EqixDuY6fOyegp3y5oqNoXacQzoz4eA82rZFxtfJEuvhGdwrs68ev4b35tEOCWHDnX5BpA%3D%3D) | |
---
Peter - Saturday marked two-years since President Trump withdrew America from the Paris Climate Accord - an international agreement that our nations would work toward shared goals to reduce carbon emissions.
This was a significant decision as it marked a turning point for American leadership where our country backed out of our commitments to work with our allies to heal the damage done to our planet.

It left our long-time allies wondering what happened to our leadership.

The fact that mankind is having a measurable impact on Earth's climate is undeniable by modern science. But that doesn't mean that we shouldn't have a spirited debate about what we can do about it.

I recently penned a column laying out a realistic approach to address climate change. [You can read it here](http://links.johnkasich.com/u/click?_t=6a3b28686d0a4d77958e12349305a4fe&_m=9b757025c5fc4b13a928493bb3349211&_e=LnIBVQq_c_-DzD0YV6REK8WU-ArPLoELEwdgwZnA7aPt3Q3k8U-i0-pyyKyBw0qpdqHSpZhUFaiJsef8RSvb1H-8uHuFYeR1y4WWBxiyFrAyQwErM-K9LaZDo5-7N8HgbeKPjPyxLr4G03K93UNGiE_MGc0vLGA0EP01ZAKH9-1Y_T9365g6SeOclWr6fGXoMezVf_EG4cPYAg2Ly26UVmCWQXMk-cOieajcNXlh7CM5hJnchCytUDUN0oVYVMLXzd_ZH4fTHXVYRwtIWJz0-xMfFt4aWagkleP42Mg4d2N6zUCL5l5j8hNbkJnrYSS-Da87zkIhteTM7UtoV_2dVrcfTO6I3cAZGoOnSbxzhd0rs7SJlk_MQd2_t2C4xQTyFqGJntGl-JAspsiEi_rtfQ%3D%3D).

**There was a time, not too long ago, when the Republican Party was the party of big ideas** , where we saw challenges for what they were and offered a conservative vision that unleashed the best of America.

Saturday was not a milestone to be proud about, but it is an opportunity to point out that retreating from big challenges is not the American way.

Our allies are looking to us as a moral compass and it's up to each of us to fight to ensure that our leaders understand the great power of the American people to fight for what is right.

It's up to each of us and together, we can make a real difference.

-John
|
---

clean_body: Saturday was not a milestone to be proud about, but it is an opportunity to point out that retreating from big challenges is not the American way | | Peter - Saturday marked two-years since President Trump withdrew America from the Paris Climate Accord - an international agreement that our nations would work toward shared goals to reduce carbon emissions. This was a significant decision as it marked a turning point for American leadership where our country backed out of our commitments to work with our allies to heal the damage done to our planet. It left our long-time allies wondering what happened to our leadership. The fact that mankind is having a measurable impact on Earth's climate is undeniable by modern science. But that doesn't mean that we shouldn't have a spirited debate about what we can do about it. I recently penned a column laying out a realistic approach to address climate change. . There was a time, not too long ago, when the Republican Party was the party of big ideas , where we saw challenges for what they were and offered a conservative vision that unleashed the best of America. Saturday was not a milestone to be proud about, but it is an opportunity to point out that retreating from big challenges is not the American way. Our allies are looking to us as a moral compass and it's up to each of us to fight to ensure that our leaders understand the great power of the American people to fight for what is right. It's up to each of us and together, we can make a real difference. -John | P.S. Peter, we say this in campaign headquarters all the time: **Our supporters are incredible. Thanks to you all, we reached our May goal and are going into June with the resources and grassroots support we need to be successful.**

You all are the backbone of your communities.You use your voices to speak up and speak out. I know the value of your hard-earned dollars, which is why we want you to know how much it means to us when you choose to use them in support of our campaign.

It's a great honor to be in this with you, and we want to keep making you proud. Will you take a moment to share some feedback with our team? We all need to be able to count on one another to get through the tough times. That's the kind of administration we want to bring back to the White House.

**We have the first debate coming up this month. So tell us, Peter, what issue is most important to you? How can our team address the topics that matter most to you?**

[We want our campaign to have these answers from you, Peter. Can you fill out our survey? Team Joe

---

Friend,
I’m currently in the green room at CNN and am moments away from taking the stage for my first nationally televised town hall.
This is a huge day for me, for the staff, and for our full team of supporters around the country because this will be the largest platform we’ve had to get our message out to voters. And honestly, friend, I’m so excited to share our ideas for the future with the entire country.
We’ll have a chance tonight to put service and security front and center of the national political conversation. To talk about uniting the country behind a common call to serve. And to talk about restoring our country as a strong, moral leader in the world.
Perhaps the most important thing we’ll be able to do tonight is to give voice to issues that no one else is talking about--like national security and mental health.
Donald Trump conducts foreign policy by Twitter, which leads to potential conflicts that risk American lives, tariffs that we end up paying for at the grocery store, and regression on climate change that affects the whole planet. We need to take him on where he’s weakest--as commander in chief--and show him what a safe, strong America really looks like. That’s what I’ll do tonight.
I’ll also be talking about mental health, the issue our campaign has been focused on in the last week. Nobody should be afraid to talk about mental health challenges or therapy in America, no more than they’re afraid to talk about physical illness or going to the gym. And ending that stigma starts by talking about these issues on the world stage, at events like this, to normalize them for all Americans.
So I hope you tune in. Knowing that I have you and an incredible team behind me means the world to me. I hope I make you proud tonight.
Thank you,
Seth

body:

Because of an incredible outpouring of support from grassroots donors across the country, we surpassed our May goal.

**We 're doing this together, and we can't build this campaign without you.**

You're making it possible for Elizabeth to keep rolling out bold new plans that will level the playing field for working people. Because of your help, she's out on the road meeting with people, sharing her story, and building our movement for big, structural change.

You're sending a message to the rich and powerful who run the show in Washington: Change is coming. Sooner than they think.

And most importantly, you're making it possible for our team to keep growing and investing in grassroots organizing -- without any private fundraisers just for wealthy donors, and without any money from federal lobbyists or PACs of any kind.

That's how we'll win. And we're just getting started.

Thanks for everything,

Team Warren
